summary: Compared the user's OS implementable improvement plan against the current DinoBrain repository/data state using direct inspection plus 10 subagent read-only audits. Identified current strengths and gaps across full-memory audit, status freshness, live hooks, agent parity, authority gates, mutation/lineage, review/semantic settlement, RAG quality, Korean UI, release/new-PC recovery, multi-PC sync, task lifecycle, and final readiness.
decisions: Current DinoBrain is a partial OS with working local memory rails, not yet the final fail-closed compounding OS described by the document.
Highest gaps are live pre-response proof robustness, agent parity status, real semantic retrieval, authority/mutation/lineage gates, release/new-PC/multi-PC recovery safety, and Korean evidence UI.
next_steps: Fix malformed/append-prone operations index and hook concurrency failures.
Implement direct MCP parity status artifact and robust live-hook verifier.
Add release artifact manifest/new-PC recovery and multi-PC safe sync gates.
Replace local hashing hybrid claims with true semantic embedding provider or honest local-vector naming.
